prompt:
Implement a design refresh for the flux app. Drop the 4-theme picker entirely; the app should now have only light and dark modes.

## Design source

Use /home/patrick/Downloads/flux-style.css as the design brief — its header comment says 'Single coherent design with dark/light only'. Treat it as a starting point you should freely refine, not a literal drop-in. The previous attempt to drop it in raw failed because the existing app/tests still assume 4 themes; this PR should embrace the redesign end-to-end.

## Scope (you should modify all of these as needed)

1. **style.css** — replace with the new CSS. Refine if you find gaps (e.g. things the new CSS removed that the app actually still uses).

2. **index.html** — remove the 4-theme picker UI (.theme-grid and the four .theme-option divs). Keep the dark/light toggle. Audit the rest of the markup for any other references to themes that no longer exist.

3. **app.js** — remove the theme-switching logic for the 4 themes (cyberpunk/material/ocean/ember). Keep the dark/light mode toggle that writes to localStorage. The localStorage key for the old theme can either be cleaned up on load (preferred) or simply ignored. Don't break version display or other settings-modal features.

4. **tests/** — update the Playwright test suite. Tests that assert 4 distinct theme accents, click .theme-option, or check 'cyberpunk uses monospace' should be deleted or rewritten to match the new single-design dark/light model. Tests for dark mode bg color should be updated to the new oklch value (or made tolerant — e.g. assert the body has data-mode='dark' rather than exact RGB). The dark/light toggle, settings modal, version display, and core app behavior should all still have coverage.

## Acceptance criteria

- All Playwright tests pass: 'nix develop -c npm test' (or whatever the project uses — check README/package.json/flake.nix).
- Manually scan index.html and app.js for any remaining references to 'cyberpunk', 'material', 'ocean', 'ember', or 'basement_lab_theme' (old localStorage key) and clean them up.
- The settings modal should still open, the dark/light toggle should work, and version info should still display.
- No dead CSS — if you remove markup, also remove styles that exclusively targeted that markup (or note clearly in the PR that you intentionally left them).
- PR title: 'design: refresh UI and drop multi-theme picker for dark/light only'
- PR body: brief explanation of the redesign, list of removed features (4 themes), confirmation that the Deploy Flux Preview workflow will publish a preview at https://flux-preview-<PR>.fly.dev/ for visual review.

## Latitude

Treat this as a design brief: feel free to tweak the new CSS where it conflicts with what the app actually needs, fix obvious bugs, and resolve test/markup mismatches in whichever direction makes the result more coherent. If something in the new CSS looks wrong (e.g. an unused selector, a likely typo), fix it. Use judgment.

Reference: previous attempt is at /tmp/klaus-sessions/flux/20260425-2009-7b6e for context on what failed — it identified that .theme-grid hidden, 4 themes collapsed to 1 accent, font-sans changed, dark bg color changed. Those are the design intent, not bugs.
